resources.string: Hooks the system framework to auto-detect and silence notification channels matching keywords like "marketing". Supports regex and batch regex rules, with built-in safe mode and hook logging.
Notes: • Switch ON = block that channel\'s notifications, OFF = allow. • A manual switch is a per-channel override — it beats the regex rules. • Hook log: %1$s/hook.log
Choose the display language. The app restarts after switching.
Root not granted — the module may not work properly
Fine-tune per-app notification channels with regex and per-channel overrides.

Safe mode is a protection that trips automatically when System UI keeps crashing. Once you\'ve confirmed your block rules are fixed, clearing the flag restores blocking.
The module watches this flag from inside the system process, so it usually recovers in place without a reboot. If it does not recover after a while, reboot once manually.
Cleared. Blocking will resume automatically, no reboot needed.
Also checks the channel description for a more complete match.

Enter one regex rule per line (use | within a line to match multiple keywords)
Examples: .*营销.* .*(推广|促销|优惠).* ^ads?_.*

Matches a notification\'s title/text and blocks the whole notification — useful for marketing pushed on a shared or "default" channel that channel-level blocking cannot reach. Applies to every notification; after updating the module, reboot once to load the hook, then switches and rule edits take effect instantly. Uses its own rules below, and is protected by the same allow-whitelist above; foreground-service notifications are never blocked. Off by default — enable with care.
Matches content when a notification is enqueued (experimental).

Content-block regex, one rule per line (use | within a line to match multiple keywords)
Example: .*(限时特惠|内购|直播间).*
When off, shows every channel the hook has recorded.
Multi-select mode: batch buttons apply only to the %1$d checked item(s)
Normal mode: batch buttons apply to the %1$d currently shown item(s)
No data yet. Tap refresh after an app creates a notification channel; if still empty, check root read access.
No channels currently match the regex rules. Turn off "Show only regex-matched channels" to see everything.
Status: %1$s Custom regex: %2$d · Built-in default: 1 · Whitelist: %3$d · App whitelist: %4$d · Overrides: %5$d Content-level blocking: %6$s · Content rules: %7$d
No blocks yet. Once blocks happen, they will be ranked here by app.
Reset the content-block count and per-app ranking to zero? This does not affect your rules or switches.
Saved and synced to the hook
Saved locally, but syncing to the hook failed — check root authorization
Failed to sync to %1$s — check root authorization
Matched channels are never blocked — this takes priority over the block rules.
Example: .*(验证码|动态密码).* .*(微信|QQ|短信).*
Saved locally, but syncing failed — check root authorization
Notifications from listed apps are never blocked (channel-level and content-level).
No apps added yet. Tap "Add App" to pick apps to fully allow.
These options are for troubleshooting only — no need to enable them for daily use.
⚠ Enabling log output writes to a file on every channel event, which may slightly affect performance. Turn it off when you\'re done troubleshooting.
When on, hook events are written to hook.log. Off by default.
When on, no logs are sent to the LSPosed module log
Only logs at or above the selected level are sent to LSPosed
